title: Why Gen Z are dominating the plastic surgery market.

segments.text: 75 of plastic surgeons saw a spike in clients under 30. so this week we are going to be discussing as due to plastic surgery and how they are changing in different age groups to help you make sense of it in your clinics as somebody who is at the lower end of millennial and going into gen Z my social media feeds are absolutely filled with young girls showing off the procedures they're getting showing off their Botox showing off that they actually in the chair getting lip filler and it just made me think like this is not normal and not only are they putting it on Tick Tock it's been glorified like they are glorifying this I am getting Botox look at this you didn't talk about the botox you got 20 years ago biggest difference is the affordability and the access to those procedures has opened them up to a younger market and younger people are not on they're not defensive they tend to be on the offensive so these girls that you're describing sound like people

who are like yeah here I am I've got the money I've Got The Power I'm having my procedure I'm getting more beautiful don't get in my way versus oh I'm getting older I'm falling apart I'm trying to keep it together a bit longer and try and maintain because that's the defensive side of it essentially becomes a status symbol and a statement of power it just means something different it doesn't have the same meaning as it used to some of that is also to do with the fact that the old version of this the Hollywood yeah plastic surgery was also people with wealth and power so as it becomes more accessible there's an element of that communication as well which is a state symbol in fact one of my patients said to me it's the new Gucci handbag yeah because it's and in fact looking over treated because looking like you've had Villa is another way of saying look I've got money and power and all I hear go get the money that gives a status to certain people so it's actually a goal to look treated by some and to be seen to be treated because of people like the Kardashians and yeah I agree with that I also think you know the way you talked about control do you genuinely think for those young girls we're saying like yeah

like 2021 it is about control or do you think it is just I've seen that person that influencer on Instagram show off their Botox and they're getting a lot of attention for it and they're getting lots of praise and positive comments about how good they look I want that too yeah I mean there's an element of buying a story with whatever you buy I often think a useful way of understanding why people do things why people purchase anything but if you're a follower of Kim Kardashian and well Chloe all those Kardashian types who I don't actually follow they represent a lot of things a lot of people would like yeah so if you take some big part of their story and replicate it you feel a bit like a little Junior version of the Kardashians and it doesn't really matter whether you get the same result or not it's like you're living some aspect of it and it probably plays out in other ways too it's very easy to be judgmental and say oh they're all being tricked and fooled and but I imagine that if you feel a certain way because you've done it act like that you have more confidence afterwards you probably do more things you wouldn't

have done if you were just a regular nobody in your mind I would say because that's not how I would describe them but in their own mind the choices become would be am I going to be just a regular nobody doing nothing or I'm going to be doing something exciting and adventurous and glamorous and if you become that person then you behave differently generally it becomes part of your identity to be out there like playing the game as opposed to hiding at home wishing you could you know it's some it's something like that which might be an element of this and it's very easy to condemn it all and say these young beautiful people need nothing and you could definitely make that argument for a huge number of them objectively yeah but it's not really about the physical like it's about that story like what are you playing full out given the environment you find yourself in there's tons of articles about young people so gen Z are leaning into Botox injectables gen Z plastic surgery craze 75 of plastic surgeons saw a spike in clients under 30. that's young man first were the most with the generation you've seen the most of ourselves like there's no

mistaking that you know don't forget a few hundred years ago you couldn't even find a mirror yeah like so then we had mirrors and now we have then they were just basically phones that people took pictures then the whole selfie thing and then we got Zoom which is which you constantly have to stare at yourself which really is like for me Zoom is the most frequent part of the day where i'm confronted with reality because in the mirror I'm I can compose myself like you look straight at yourself you're not kind of looking at different angles and trying to speak and all that stuff wakes you up to even more reality so if you have an issue with a bump on your nose it's confronting you for three hours every day yeah like it never used to be like that you could forget about it when someone paid you a compliment even 20 years ago it was only ever done either on the phone or to you face to face whereas people are now seeing other people getting compliments all over the place on their which makes me think oh they're getting compliments why aren't I getting compliments It's

like ballooned because everyone just used to get compliments in their own little bubble but now everyone's seeing everyone's compliments the hidden Sinister element is that most of those images are not even real anyway which I'll be honest over many of those pictures in the early days with the whole you know Kardashian stuff that I assumed that they were just really good looking because they'd had really good plastic surgeons and then you have to see the other view of them leaving the clinic with a non-filtered image to be like oh they just looked like they did before but I could see something's happen but they all they still have it it's no longer looks so perfect so people compare themselves with an illusion and then it's so obvious what gets attention that you almost can't shut off that I that voice in your head that says pursue this like try and get some of this magic which would be fine if it was a real thing I think it's sad if it's a mirage like you're not actually ever going to get there was also this thing so there's an influencer called Ellie Rallo and she was so trolled in the comments about her gummy smile that she ended up getting a lip flip and then to me that posed the question like would she have got a lip flip if she wasn't an

influencer and did that trolling drive her to get that lip flip or do you think she would have gotten it anyway even if she wasn't influenced she probably wouldn't have but she wouldn't have but it's not it's that's for me that's not an ethical issue because as a doctor you're trying to empower people in their context have you ever met someone with some distracting issue and just not been a struggled not to think about it so I guess so but it said anything and I've tried like I've still listened to that person like it hasn't distracted me to the point that I can no longer like that Austin Powers thing which one the gold member with the stick and the mole by the way I realized that I have a large mole on my face where if no one else had thoughts that enter your mind when you meet some of the distraction like that it just clip would mean nothing so what comedy does is it reframes how you look at a situation

and often makes us conscious of what we're doing intuitively we understand like it's very I remember having a mole cut of my nose and having a little plaster there was this girl I liked at the time for the whole time I had that plaster I used to walk the back way around the school that actually breaks my heart that breaks my heart because she probably wouldn't have given yeah but I felt like I had this enormous glaring distraction on my face and I couldn't connect with her and she'd just see me as that weird like bleeding face boy you know I think instinctively we understand that your face is for communication and if you have something on your face that occupies someone's brain in a different way as opposed to what you're trying to say then you lose something and so we have an instinct to try and get those distractions those barriers out the way so we can connect with people now it's not that P that people is not necessarily because the people who are saying mean things there are two reasons why you say mean things on the internet one is you're looking for status and power yourself you've got none so you come on and say well you might have all these followers but you've got gummy smile whatever but the other one is a genuine distraction and they kind of just blurt it out like

as in maybe there's an element of both I suppose but they will be saying something that they are noticing instead of what she's saying so you I would separate the two issues it's not okay to go online and say mean things to people and you wouldn't do it but I would say that you probably would also notice things about people ahead of the message they're trying to get across in certain circumstances and that's why people are trying to get rid of they're trying to create friction free connection and communication her context is different and so her needs are different and if that is helped to get her message across because what she's also this is an issue for most people with appearances really appearances about connection it's about being able to connect with people strongly so that you can collaborate with them that's the Baseline of why we want to be more beautiful connection collaboration she has essentially picked up that people are not connecting with her because of a gummy smile instead of listening to what she's saying they're saying oh look at your gummy smile so she removes that she has one less obstacle to connect with people it's an appropriate procedure You could argue that it's sad and people should just be kind but if they're all thinking it anyway and they can't they're not listening to her they're looking at a gummy smile they've actually just told

her the truth of what's going on she's made a correction now she gets her message across in return better this is where there's a conflict because we also on one level you want people to be happy in their skin and not to live in some sort of emotional pain of not being enough but on another level if it genuinely is something that stops people thinking about what you're saying because they're too busy looking at whatever imperfection then and they let it slip that that's how they feel and then you get it corrected you do actually get some additional power for that you get that attention you get that connection with people you could argue it's just a system at play and we're part of a system of feedback and response yeah but well the issue was people being cruel in comments so I would never go on someone's Tick Tock and write something mean about the way they looked there's two have you not noticed there's two categories especially with younger people and I'm sure you can see it clashing on social media when people do post stuff about the botox they're getting they'll also get a backlash from people from younger people who 100 don't agree with it and think it's unnecessary

and trivial and shallow and yeah absolutely so these are all the stories that people tell in order to make decisions and they rewrite the story often to solve an emotional Clash you know the story of I'm really natural I really love natural things like I often have people who having just about to Botox filler who will say all that stuff so they want a natural looking result they're still having it they're just having a natural result because they're very natural and then other people who would tell a different story which is you know I'm not here to I'm here to make the most of my life and I'm not I'm gonna not gonna get old and I'm going to fight it in every way possible because that's one of my values and yeah I'll have the organic but I'm also controlling my appearance as much as I can I'm not giving in to Nature so let us know what you think about this interesting topic and don't forget to like And subscribe so that the algorithm serves you more videos just like this thanks for watching [Music]
